tool.name: Sex.DetERRmine
tool.command:
## 1. Environment Setup ln -s '$input_bam' input.bam && ln -sf '$input_bam.metadata.bam_index' input.bam.bai &&
## 2. BED Logic Selection #if $input_bed #set $bed_file = $input_bed #else #set $bed_file = $fallback_bed #end if
## 3. Generate depth samtools depth -H -aa -q $base_quality -Q $map_quality -b '$bed_file' input.bam > depth_final.txt &&
## 4. Execute Sex.DetERRmine using the final formatted file sexdeterrmine -I depth_final.txt > '$output_report'

configfiles.configfile: #set $valid = ['21', '22', 'X', 'Y', 'chr21', 'chr22', 'chrX', 'chrY'] #if $input_bam.metadata.reference_names and $input_bam.metadata.reference_lengths #set $names = str($input_bam.metadata.reference_names).replace('__ob__','').replace('__cb__','').replace('__sq__','').replace(' ','').split(',') #set $lengths = str($input_bam.metadata.reference_lengths).replace('__ob__','').replace('__cb__','').replace(' ','').split(',') #for $n, $l in zip($names, $lengths) #if $n in $valid ${n} 0 ${l} #end if #end for #end if

param.name: input_bed
param.label: Target Regions (BED) [Optional]
param.help: BED file defining the genomic regions to calculate coverage. Leave empty to use full-length chromosomes

param.name: base_quality
param.label: Base Quality (q)
param.help: Minimum base quality required to consider a read.

param.name: map_quality
param.label: Mapping Quality (Q)
param.help: Minimum mapping quality required to consider a read.

tool.help:
**Sex.DetERRmine: Sex Determination from Low-Coverage Sequencing Data**
This tool calculates the relative coverage of sex chromosomes (X and Y) compared to autosomes to determine the biological sex of a sample. It is particularly useful for ancient DNA (aDNA) and other low-depth sequencing data.
**How it works:**
1. **Coverage Calculation:** It uses `samtools depth` to calculate the per-base coverage across specified regions. 2. **Sex Rates:** It calculates two primary metrics: - **Rx (X-rate):** The ratio of X-chromosome coverage to autosomal coverage. - **Ry (Y-rate):** The ratio of Y-chromosome coverage to autosomal coverage.
**Reference Detection:**
With a user-provided BED dataset, the tool will recognize sex chromosome regions by their chromosome name (looking for X or chrX and Y or chrY). Every other region is treated as an autosomal one.
If no BED dataset is provided, the tool inspects the BAM header metadata looking for
1. sex chromosome names
2. the names (chr)21 and (chr)22, which it will use as representative autosomes
It will then analyze these sequences across their entire length.
**Outputs:**
A tabular report including the calculated x- and y-rates, and their associated error values.
**Example Interpretation:**
========== ========== ==================== Metric Value Interpretation ---------- ---------- -------------------- x-rate ~1.0 XX (Female) y-rate ~0.0 XX (Female) x-rate ~0.5 XY (Male) y-rate >0.1 XY (Male) ========== ========== ====================
